strengths: ~20 years of broad software engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded systems, and cloud infrastructure
Entrepreneurial track record — founded GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform in active use by NGOs) and has startup exposure via WatchUp
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing through public conference talks (FOSS4G 2019) and multi-day workshops on testing and web internals
concerns: AI expertise is negligible — only two months of vague AI-agent tooling work at re:cinq; no ML background, no AI products shipped at scale, no evidence of AI strategy ownership
No director-level IC or strategic advisory experience — the entire career is execution-focused individual contribution with no documented influence over product direction or customer strategy
Current title is Senior Software Developer, two levels below the Principal/Director-level IC the role requires; no Master's or PhD in an AI-related field
response.justification: The role's primary differentiator is demonstrated AI strategy and product leadership at a director-level IC scope. Against that bar, the candidate falls significantly short: the only AI-related evidence is a two-month-old bullet point about MCP code generators, with no shipped AI products, no ML depth, and no history of advising customers or shaping technical strategy. Cloud exposure exists (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) but is incidental rather than deep. The candidate's ~20-year engineering breadth and open-source founding experience are genuine strengths but do not compensate for the core competency gaps the role demands.
